fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 46-year-old African American male with schizophrenia with ongoing symptoms.
fields.Subjective: Mr. Foster attended appointment with case manager. Case manager confirms he takes quetiapine 300 mg twice daily via supervised administration at day program, no missed doses. Despite good adherence, continues experiencing moderate symptoms. Reports daily auditory hallucinations of voices discussing him. Endorses vague paranoid ideas about surveillance. Sleep disrupted at 5-6 hours nightly. Appetite fair, weight stable at 187 lbs. Attends day program three days weekly. Not working, receives disability. Lives in supported housing. Limited social contacts. Mood 'okay' rated 5/10. Denies substance use. Flat during interview. Safety: No SI/HI. (Assessed by Sandra Williams, LCSW, Licensed Clinical Social Worker)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Middle-aged male, adequate grooming, appears withdrawn

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives in supported housing; unemployed, on disability; attends day program; case management services.
Assessment_and_Plan: Will increase quetiapine to 400 mg BID.
fields.Author: Note written by Sandra Williams, LCSW, Licensed Clinical Social Worker — Community Living Services, 5623 Housing Way, Oakland, CA 94607, Phone: (555) 772-4498, Fax: (555) 772-4499
